title: Fix Tenant Isolation and Cross-Tenant Authorization Vulnerability
description:
Critical security fix for a tenant isolation vulnerability. Users could view data from other tenants by simply modifying the account ID in the URL, bypassing all authorization checks.

The fix involved:
1. Removing the account ID from URLs entirely (routing to `/myaccounts` instead of `/myaccounts/{id}`)
2. Dynamically resolving the authenticated user's account from the session rather than from URL parameters
3. Removing legacy code that relied on URL-based account identification
4. Adding server-side validation that only the account owner can perform administrative actions (invite, manage permissions) — any attempt to inject a different ID returns an error.
acceptance_criteria: Users cannot view other tenants' data by modifying URL parameters
Account context is derived from the authenticated session, not URL parameters
Only the account owner can perform invite and permission management actions
Attempts to inject a foreign account ID return an error
Legacy code using URL-based account lookup is removed
middleware_changes: Account routing changed from URL parameter-based to session-based. Owner ID is resolved dynamically for the authenticated user. Server-side validation prevents unauthorized access to other tenants' accounts and data.
implementation_notes: This was flagged as a critical security vulnerability. The fix removed all URL-based account ID references and replaced them with session-based account resolution. Legacy code was also cleaned up during this fix.
comment_summary: Developer fixed the vulnerability by removing the account ID from URLs, switching to session-based account resolution, and removing legacy code. Product confirmed appreciation for the legacy code cleanup.
